entry.title: Procedure For End-To-End Encryption And Signing Of File Through SFTP Via Integration Suite PART-2
entry.content:
I have a requirement where I must process a payment file generated in SAP through integration suite by encrypting and signing the file, sending the file to the bank server. We are sending and receiving the payment file through SFTP, to connect to the bank server, we have provided them with a PUBLIC SSH KEY. We were given the public key of the receiver, folder path to place the file, server address and credentials of the same to login and we must process the file generated in SAP to the receiver by encrypting and signing the file. We should encrypt the data using receiver’s public key and sign the same using our private key. I have discussed the keys part in my first blog in this blog i would like to share the details regarding integration flow part, if any one have missed any details regarding the first part I am sharing the link here for your access to the first blog,

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/procedure-for-end-to-end-encryption-and-signing-of-file-through-sftp-via/ba-p/13873725

Once you have successfully uploaded the keys in PGP keys and after testing the connectivity of source and target destinations, we now have configured the sender and receiver configuration, I have developed my integration flow, please find below image,

 

I had captured the file which came from SAP in the content modifier and now I have the necessary data to be encrypted and signed, let us now see the configuration of PGPEncryptor, In PGPEncryptor please go to processing and there you can find the first option where is says signatures, as shown below,

 

 

  • I must encrypt the data and sign the same, so I am including the signatures.
  • You can select the content encryption algorithm, secret key length and compression algorithm according to your requirement, I had changed the compression algorithm to uncompressed as I don’t want to compress the data.
  • There is an option of armored, if you have enabled/ticked/checked the box then the resulting PGP message will be encoded with a specific header in base64.
  • You can check the integrity protected data packet if you want to create a symmetrically encrypted and integrity protected data packet.
  • Now you can find the option of encryption key user IDs from public keyring where you must add public key of the receiver which we have earlier uploaded in PGP Keys, also find the signer key user IDs where we must add our private/secret key which we have generated using GPA tool earlier and uploaded to the PGP keys, please find the image below,

 

 

Now that you have done with configuration of encryption and signing, let me also show you the configuration for decryption as shown below,

 

 

  • In PGPDecryptor there is no configuration for the decryption unless you want to verify the signature, where you receive the data which is encrypted and signed.
  • As you are receiver in the case of decryptor you require the public key of the sender to verify the signature of the sender.

I would like to share my point that this requirement is configured for host to host configuration with bank and customer.

So, I conclude the whole requirement where we have seen generating SSH KEYS using PUTTY KEY GENERATOR, generating KEY PAIR using GPA TOOL, processing the payment file from SAP to BANK server by encrypting and signing the file.

Please provide me your valuable feedback and if you any queries feel free to share.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 3 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, October 11th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, October 14th.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

If you are looking for Week 1 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities - SAP Community

If you are looking for Week 2 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities - SAP Community

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the second week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt will be posted here later in week 3. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  This week's Fun Friday activity will be 🟡 Bugtoberfest 2024 - SAP Community

List of activities for contest week 3:
